thresholds.note: Go thresholds are lower than npm (1000x/50K) to account for ecosystem size difference. Go modules are more explicitly listed in go.mod, compressing amplification ratios.

top_packages.name: github.com/jmespath/go-jmespath/internal/testify
top_packages.note: INTERNAL package depended on externally — test helper inside go-jmespath's internal/ dir

top_packages.name: github.com/jackc/pgproto3
top_packages.note: PostgreSQL wire protocol — superseded by pgx v5

top_packages.name: dmitri.shuralyov.com/gpu/mtl
top_packages.note: Metal GPU API bindings by Go contributor Dmitri Shuralyov

top_packages.name: rsc.io/binaryregexp
top_packages.note: Regexp for binary/latin-1 data by Russ Cox (Go lead)

top_packages.name: rsc.io/quote/v3
top_packages.note: Tutorial package ('pithy sayings') used in official Go modules docs — spread via copy-paste from examples

top_packages.name: rsc.io/sampler
top_packages.note: Tutorial package ('shows simple texts') from official Go modules tutorial

top_packages.name: github.com/BurntSushi/xgb
top_packages.note: X11 protocol bindings by Andrew Gallant (ripgrep author)

top_packages.name: github.com/ianlancetaylor/demangle
top_packages.note: C++ symbol demangling by Ian Lance Taylor (Go compiler team)

top_packages.name: github.com/google/martian
top_packages.note: HTTP proxy for testing (legacy module path — v3 coexists)

top_packages.name: github.com/hashicorp/go.net
top_packages.note: Deprecated wrapper for golang.org/x/net, still pulled in transitively

key_findings.vs_npm: Go Nebraska blocks are 100-2000x amplification vs npm's 1000-10000x. Go's explicit go.mod makes transitive packages more visible, compressing ratios. Ecosystem is ~15x smaller by repo count.
key_findings.tutorial_creep: rsc.io/sampler and rsc.io/quote/v3 are official Go modules tutorial packages written by Russ Cox (Go lead) that spread into production codebases via copy-paste from examples. Combined 171K repos, 9-108 stars.
key_findings.go_core_team: Packages from Go core team members dominate: Russ Cox (rsc.io/*), Ian Lance Taylor (ianlancetaylor/demangle), Dmitri Shuralyov (dmitri.shuralyov.com/*). Personal domain packages by the people who built Go are now load-bearing infrastructure.
key_findings.internal_package_problem: github.com/jmespath/go-jmespath/internal/testify is an internal/ package (meant to be unexportable) that 41K repos depend on, likely via indirect dependency chains that pre-date Go modules enforcement.
key_findings.deprecated_packages: github.com/hashicorp/go.net (deprecated wrapper for golang.org/x/net) still runs in 58K repos at 593x amplification — same collapse pattern as npm's request ecosystem, but slower.
